title: Intensely Rich Algerian Herring Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful Algerian dish featuring herring, perfect for a hangover cure, made with double-rise-bread and served in individual mini cocottes.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions and cook until they are softened and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked onion mixture, herring fillets, double-rise-bread cubes, tomato paste, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
In a small bowl, whisk together the chicken broth and a pinch of salt.
Pour the chicken broth mixture over the herring mixture and stir until the bread is well coated.
Divide the mixture among 6 individual mini cocottes.
Cover the cocottes with lids and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the lids and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes.
